div.head: The Chargé at Tangier (Childs) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 33. Legation’s despatches No. 475Dated December 9, 1941, p. 439. and 432 [532].Not printed. My British colleague recommended to his Government that the approach mentioned in last paragraph, page 2, despatch No. 475i. e., first paragraph on p. 440. be made. He has now hinted that the situation would not appear to be propitious to attempt any but the most restrained approach at the present time without raising any present hope of aid or inviting a request for such.

The situation which has developed within the last few days in LibyaOn January 21 German forces under General Rommel in Libya began the drive to the east. appears to us all, including in particular, Colonel Eddy,Lt. Col. William A. Eddy, Naval Attaché at Tangier. to make this an inappropriate time for us to raise the subject and we are accordingly taking no action at this time.
Full air mail report despatch no. 585Not printed. leaving tomorrow.
